jobs.title: Manager, Product Planning and Development
jobs.jd_full:
Start your journey with BCD: Grow, connect, collaborate and celebrate with our global team

Manager Product Planning (Remote)

Full time, United States

The Manager Product Planning role leads the product planning efforts for assigned solutions. As global leader of related key products of the assigned solution line, the role takes full responsibility for the product road maps and their delivery globally, including development of the go-to-market approach and market penetration. The role partners with and influences stakeholders internally and externally, in order to identify, shape and deliver products which focus on increasing commercial opportunities across our client segments.

As a Manager Product Planning, you will

  • Monitor product development projects to ensure they are on-scope and on-schedule
  • Make sure your assigned products have sound business plans, including an analysis of product profitability, sales success and pricing strategy
  • Identify current market needs and possible future gaps in our offering and conduct feasibility studies to bring ideas forward
  • Conduct a competitive analysis for all your assigned products to show how we compare on the market
  • Engage with your peers across departments, support your colleagues with their projects, and reach out when you need to be supported
  • Building relationships with our vendors; listen to their thoughts and concerns and foster ways to work together

About you

  • Prior experience in business travel or travel management 
  • Knowledge of GDS Sabre and GDS Amadeus
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills. You'll interact with a variety of partners and will need a high level of comfort in communicating with professionals of different backgrounds
  • Excellent time-management and project-management skills: you'll be constantly challenged to balance multiple initiatives, projects and perspectives
  • Competency with Microsoft Office Products, and collaborative software used for projects with remote team members and stakeholders
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English

What we offer you

At BCD Travel, we work with highly motivated and passionate people. We value open communication, collaboration, and a flexible work-life balance. We offer unique, often global, experiences that empower you to develop and grow within the organization. Sustainability and helping others is also high on our list. We make a difference through charitable activities in our communities across the globe.

About us

BCD Travel helps companies travel smart and achieve more. We drive program adoption, cost savings and talent retention through digital experiences that simplify business travel. Our 15,000+ dedicated team members service clients in 170+ countries as we shape a sustainable future for business travel. BCD’s leading meetings and events management and global consultancy services complete our comprehensive suite of solutions for all aspects of corporate travel. In 2024, BCD achieved US$22.9 billion in sales. For more information, visit www.bcdtravel.com.

Get to know us by reading our blog and checking out our social media:

  • https://www.bcdtravel.com/blog/
  • https://www.linkedin.com/company/bcd-travel/
  • https://www.instagram.com/bcdtravel/
  • https://www.facebook.com/bcdtravel/

You’ll be offered

  • Flexible working hours and work-from-home or remote opportunities
  • Opportunities to grow your skillset and career
  • Generous vacation days so you can rest and recharge
  • A compensation package that feels fair to you, including mental, physical, and financial wellbeing tools
  • Travel industry professional perks and discounts
  • An inclusive work environment where diversity is celebrated

At BCD Travel we value you, our culture and success is defined by you. We carefully consider a wide range of compensation factors, including your professional background and experience. The salary range for candidates in applicable jurisdictions in the US for the position of Manager Product Planning is between $104,000 and $118,000. The actual pay depends on your skills, qualifications, experience and geographical location. 

Ready to join the journey? Apply now!

We’re dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about a role, but your experience doesn’t align perfectly, we still encourage you to apply.

jobs.title: Director, Product Management - Global Store Discovery
jobs.jd_full:
Job Summary: 

The Director, Product Management – Global Store Discovery will lead the global digital shopping discovery experience across desktop web, mobile web, and native apps. This role will own the strategy, roadmap execution, experimentation, and cross-functional delivery for the core discovery surfaces that connect customers with products across iHerb’s global platform. Key areas of ownership include the Homepage, Search (including AI-powered search), Product Listing Pages (PLPs), Navigation, Promotional and Campaign landing experiences, Personalized discovery feeds, and other browse and discovery surfaces across the site and app.

Job Expectations:

Product Strategy & Vision

  • Define and drive the global product vision, strategy, and roadmap for store discovery experiences across desktop, mobile web, and native apps.

  • Build differentiated discovery experiences that improve product findability, browsing engagement, conversion, and customer retention.

  • Develop customer-centric strategies tailored for both new and returning users across the full discovery journey.

  • Identify emerging trends in AI-powered search, personalization, recommendation engines, and competitive discovery experiences.

Product Ownership Areas

Homepage

  • Own the global homepage experience as the primary brand and discovery entry point.

  • Drive strategy for content layout, personalization, promotional placement, featured categories, localization and curated product surfaces.

  • Optimize for engagement, new user acquisition, and returning customer activation across all devices.

  • Partner with Merchandising and Marketing to enable scalable content operations and campaign execution.

Search

  • Own the end-to-end search frontend product experience.

  • Drive AI/ML-powered search improvements including semantic search, vector-based retrieval and AI driven shopping experiences.

  • Partner with Data Science and Engineering to continuously improve search quality metrics and conversion rates from search.

  • Support multilingual and global search experiences across all iHerb markets.

Product Listing Pages (PLPs)

  • Own PLP experiences across category pages, brand pages, promotion pages, and search results pages.

  • Optimize product card layouts, sorting and filtering options, pagination, and information hierarchy.

  • Drive personalization of PLP ranking and product sequencing based on behavioral and contextual signals.

  • Improve mobile-first browsing and grid/list display experiences.

  • Partner with Merchandising on promotional badging, deals visibility, and category curation strategies.

Navigation & Site Structure

  • Own global navigation architecture including primary nav, mega-menus, breadcrumbs, and category taxonomy.

  • Drive improvements in site hierarchy, interlinking, and navigational discoverability.

  • Support SEO-driven structural improvements including internal linking strategies and URL architecture.

  • Ensure navigation scales across international markets with localization and regional category structures.

Promotional & Campaign Discovery Surfaces

  • Own discovery surfaces for promotions, deals, seasonal events, and campaign landing pages.

  • Drive strategy for sale pages, deals hubs, clearance surfaces, and time-limited offer discovery.

  • Partner with Marketing and Merchandising to ensure campaign experiences are optimized for conversion and scale.

Leadership & Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Lead and mentor a team of Product Managers and potentially Product Owners across store discovery domains.

  • Drive strong collaboration across Engineering, Design, Analytics, Data Science, Marketing, SEO, Merchandising, and Operations teams.

  • Establish product development best practices, experimentation rigor, and KPI accountability across all discovery surfaces.

  • Communicate product vision, roadmap priorities, and business impact to executive stakeholders.

Data, Analytics & Experimentation

Define and monitor KPIs including:

  • Search conversion rate and search click-through rate

  • PLP engagement and add-to-cart rates

  • Homepage engagement and click-through metrics

  • Bounce rate and session depth from discovery surfaces

  • Revenue per visitor and revenue per search

  • Discovery-driven AOV and units per transaction

  • Personalization lift and recommendation performance

  • Time-to-product-found and navigation funnel efficiency

  • Champion A/B testing and data-driven decision-making across all discovery touchpoints.

  • Leverage customer insights, behavioral analytics, search analytics, and market research to prioritize initiatives.

The duties and responsibilities described above may provide only a partial description of this position. This is not an exhaustive list of all aspects of the job. Other duties and responsibilities not outlined in this document may be added as necessary or desirable, with or without notice.

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:

Required:

  • Proven experience owning large-scale customer-facing digital discovery experiences across web and mobile platforms.

  • Strong expertise in ecommerce search, browse, navigation, personalization, and conversion optimization.

  • Deep understanding of search relevance, ranking systems, filtering/faceting, and AI-powered search technologies.

  • Experience leading global product initiatives across multiple international markets.

  • Demonstrated success managing cross-functional teams and influencing executive stakeholders.

  • Strong analytical skills with experience using experimentation frameworks and product analytics tools.

  • Excellent communication, prioritization, and organizational leadership skills.

  • High degree of accuracy and attention to detail

  • Excellent organization skills and ability to multi-task

Preferred: 

  • Experience in high-growth ecommerce, retail, marketplace, or consumer technology companies with global scale.

  • Familiarity with AI/ML-powered search, recommendation systems, and personalization platforms.

  • Experience with mobile app ecosystems and mobile discovery optimization.

  • Knowledge of SEO best practices including site architecture, structured data, and content discoverability.

  • Experience with merchandising systems, promotional platforms, and content management at scale.

Equipment Knowledge:

  • Is there any equipment that is must to know

  • Experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

  • Experience with Google Business Suite (Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Forms) preferred

Experience Requirements:

Generally requires 12+ years of Product Management experience, including leadership roles in e-commerce, digital retail, or consumer technology platforms with a focus on discovery, search, or shopping experiences.

Education Requirements: 

Bachelor’s Degree required. MBA or advanced degree preferred.

Judgment/Reasoning Ability: Able to identify, troubleshoot and resolve problems quickly using sound judgment, poise and diplomacy. Ability to use judgment and reasoning skills, and determine when to escalate issues, as required, in a timely manner.

Physical Demands: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by a Team Member to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the Team Member is regularly required to talk and hear. The Team Member is frequently required to sit, walk, climb stairs, use hands and fingers, bend, stoop and reach with hands and arms. Reaching above shoulder heights, below the waist or lifting as required to file documents or store materials throughout the work day. The Team Member may occasionally lift or move office products and supplies up to 25 pounds. Proper lifting techniques required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Work Environment: The noise in the work environment is usually moderate. Other factors are:

  • Hectic, fast-paced with multi-level distractions

  • Professional, yet casual work environment

  • Office / Warehouse environment

  • Ability to work extended hours as required

jobs.title: Strategy and Operations Lead
jobs.jd_full:
About PatientIQ

PatientIQ is a fast-growing health tech company on a mission to improve patient outcomes. We partner with healthcare providers, medical device companies, and researchers to collect, analyze, and act on patient-reported outcomes data at scale â powering smarter clinical decisions and accelerating the future of value-based care.

Headquartered in Chicago and backed by top-tier venture capital and major health system investors, PatientIQ is building the largest platform for healthcare providers, industry partners, and researchers to collaborate on what matters most: helping patients get better.

The opportunity

PatientIQ is hiring a Strategy & Operations Lead to help drive the company's highest-priority strategic initiatives and strengthen the operating foundation required for our next phase of growth.

Working directly with the Chief of Staff and executive leadership team, you'll help build and scale our Strategic Program Office (SPO) - a new function responsible for governing the company's portfolio of strategic initiatives while improving how we plan, communicate, and execute across the business.

This role is ideal for a highly analytical operator who thrives in ambiguity, brings structure to complex problems, and enjoys turning strategy into execution.

What You'll Do

  • Own PatientIQ's portfolio of strategic initiativesâgoverning the company's highest-priority work from prioritization through execution, ensuring every initiative has clear ownership, measurable outcomes, and executive visibility
  • Lead complex cross-functional initiatives from strategy through execution, partnering across Product, Commercial, Customer Success, Engineering, Finance, and Operations to deliver measurable business results
  • Help build and scale PatientIQ's Strategic Program Office (SPO) by establishing the governance, operating cadence, portfolio management, and accountability mechanisms that enable the company to execute at scale
  • Partner with the Chief of Staff and executive leadership team on strategic planning, executive operating rhythms, organizational priorities, board and leadership communications, and other high-impact initiatives critical to the business
  • Develop executive-ready analyses, recommendations, and communications that translate complex information into clear decisions, align stakeholders, and enable faster, higher-quality execution
  • Define and measure business impact by establishing KPI frameworks, tracking strategic initiatives, surfacing risks early, and ensuring investments deliver measurable operational and commercial outcomes
  • Champion AI-enabled ways of working by standardizing AI-powered workflows, automating manual processes, and embedding AI into how teams plan, communicate, analyze, and executeâcreating meaningful leverage across the organization
  • Drive accountability across the organization by clarifying ownership, facilitating decision-making, removing execution roadblocks, and maintaining momentum on the company's most important priorities

What Success Looks Like

We believe clarity drives performance. Here's what the first 90 days look like in this role:

30 days - Build strong relationships with executive stakeholders and functional leaders. Develop a deep understanding of PatientIQ's business, product, customers, strategic priorities, and operating cadence. Begin identifying opportunities to improve execution, communication, and operational efficiency. Become proficient with the company's AI tools and workflows.

60 days - Independently own one or more executive operating cadences. Lead a cross-functional strategic initiative with minimal oversight. Deliver executive-ready analyses and communications that enable faster decision-making. Begin establishing the SPO framework

90 days - Be recognized as a trusted partner to the Chief of Staff and executive team. Fully support the company's strategic initiative portfolio and executive reporting cadence. Launch the foundational components of the Strategic Program Office. Deliver measurable improvements in execution through AI-enabled workflows and standardized operating processes.

Requirements

What you bring

Required

  • 2-4 years of experience in management consulting, investment banking, private equity, corporate strategy, or business operations at a high-growth SaaS or technology company
  • Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to structure ambiguous questions, identify the most important issues, and develop clear, actionable recommendations
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, including experience producing concise, executive-ready materials independently
  • Demonstrated ability to lead complex, cross-functional initiatives and drive accountability across teams without formal authority
  • Strong business judgmentâyou understand when to analyze further, when to make a recommendation, and when to push an organization toward a decision
  • Highly organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple high-priority initiatives while maintaining a clear view of the broader business context
  • Comfort operating in a fast-moving environment where priorities evolve, information may be incomplete, and the path forward is not always defined
  • High ownership mindsetâyou do not wait for perfect direction and are comfortable building structure, defining the work, and driving it to completion
  • Strong aptitude for AI and modern productivity tools, with an interest in using technology to improve analysis, communication, and organizational efficiency
  • Exposure to go-to-market strategy, revenue operations, product strategy, healthcare technology, or B2B SaaS is a strong differentiator
  • Bachelor's degree required

Nice to have

  • Previous experience in a healthcare technology environment

Benefits

Why PatientIQ

  • Mission-driven work. We're solving a hard problem that matters â improving patient outcomes through better data. Every person here is working on something real
  • True idea meritocracy. Great ideas win, regardless of title. We encourage every team member to challenge the status quo
  • World-class team. You'll work alongside people who are at the top of their fields in health tech, enterprise software, and clinical science
  • Fast-growing. We're building the largest platform for healthcare providers and industry to collaborate â and we're just getting started
  • Great benefits. Top-notch health, dental, and vision. 401(k). Flexible PTO â we trust you to take the time you need. Professional development stipends
  • Real ownership. This is not a role where you maintain someone else's playbook. You'll build, shape strategy, and see your impact directly

jobs.title: Product Associate Director (Group Product Manager)
jobs.jd_full:
At Two Six Technologies, we build, deploy, and implement innovative products that solve the world’s most complex challenges today. Through unrivaled collaboration and unwavering trust, we push the boundaries of what’s possible to empower our team and support our customers in building a safer global future.

Advance US policy objectives with the latest technologies in Agentic AI and generative coding practices. Two Six Technologies is seeking a Product Leader with a strong building mindset to take ownership over our early-stage product portfolio for Decision Advantage. This is an ideal opportunity for an individual who wants to have significant strategic impact, while staying close to innovation in agentic AI technologies and AI-accelerated product practices.

You will be in a player-coach role, both managing an individual product backlog and also taking on strategic ownership over the broader portfolio. You will take the lead on ensuring product roadmaps arrive ahead of emerging market opportunities in:
• Enterprise agentic orchestration and data fabric
• Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear, and Explosives (CBRNE) sensor monitoring
Two Six has built a state-of-the-art agentic AI orchestration system and supports incumbent products that solve mission challenges. The balance of legacy software and state of the art agentic orchestration mirrors the realities in place for customers across the Federal Government, as well as State, Local, and International. Teams are putting the latest technology advancements to work on mission objectives in an AI system that is action-oriented, trustworthy, and human-in-the-loop.

We want to hear from you if you are:

  • A leader in exceptional product tradecraft
    • Able to coach product tradecraft and create value through investing in the skill sets of others on your team
    • A builder, passionate about the craft of creating exceptional products and applying “just now available” technologies to solve enduring customer problems
    • A strategist, able to isolate the problem that must be solved to create exceptional value for customers and improve our competitive positioning
    • Grounded in military or intelligence culture and acquisition cycles through either prior service, or work on military or intel products
    This is a fully remote position with flexibility to manage your day around collaboration with your team. DC location is preferred but not required. Some travel (10-20%) will be required to interface with our customers and Two Six employees collaborating on cleared programs.

What you will do:

  • Collaborate with sales, growth, and other go-to-market teams to understand our competitive landscape and positioning, and identify key product investments to sustain and grow market dominance for our category
    • Work with your team, customers, stakeholders, and the content team to crystalize and communicate a product portfolio vision and strategy inside and outside the company
    • Maintain at least one product roadmap and backlog, building in an empowered product team model
    • Coach product manager(s) and UX designer(s) to effectively meet strategic direction
    • Collaborate with your cross-functional leadership team and company executives to create the best possible environment for teams to succeed
    • Provide top-down direction while also taking bottom-up feedback and recommendations
    • Be a leading example of the product operating model and product best practices, and drive change towards a user-centered culture across the company where this can thrive
    • This leadership position is expected to innovate alongside teams, filling gaps where necessary. It’s an ideal mix for leaders who still want to remain builders.
    • Be open and flexible to jump in where help is needed

What you will need (basic qualifications):

  • Experience creating products for government users, particularly in environments with limitations on technology access
    • Experience with information operations, intelligence, or targeting missions and markets
    • Experience with agentic AI, generative AI, natural language processing, large language models or advanced analytics, particularly deployed in production environments at scale
    • Experience accelerating product and engineering tradecraft with AI assistance
    • Experience with B2B products with a heavy product support component or consulting practice
    • Interest in supporting US government missions to counter foreign adversary and non-state actor propaganda and disinformation

Clearance Requirement:

  • Active TS or TS/SCI Clearance is a bonus, but those without a clearance are encouraged to apply

We believe in rewarding skills, experience, and performance. jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
*Immigration / Work Authorization Notice:* Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States. iSpot is not able to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment visa for this position at this time.

iSpot competes for the best talent. Our compensation packages consist of salary and equity in one of Seattle’s hottest start-ups, as well as other standard benefits. Most importantly, we provide a really interesting working experience, and the chance to contribute to the success of something great.

What You’ll Be Part Of:

iSpot.tv is changing how brands, agencies, and networks measure and assess the impact of TV advertising. The iSpot service connects TV advertising to actual business outcomes, empowering the world’s top brands to plan, measure, and optimize their TV campaigns and to understand their influence on business growth.

Our Operations team provides mission-critical support to our sales, customer success, and marketing teams. The Technical Program Manager (TPM) will own and drive the execution of core business system initiatives, applying program management rigor—such as comprehensive requirements gathering, roadmap planning, timeline tracking, and stakeholder communication—to bridge the gap between complex business needs and technical systems architecture. This is a fast-paced, high-impact role ideal for an analytical problem solver who thrives in a collaborative environment and can lead cross-functional initiatives from inception to delivery.

Responsibilities:

●      Program Leadership: Partner closely with cross-functional business stakeholders (e.g., Sales, Marketing, Finance, Product and Engineering) to scope strategic initiatives and author technical requirements, including Product Requirement Documents (PRDs) and detailed user stories.

●      Execution & Delivery: Lead the end-to-end execution of complex, technical projects by establishing project milestones, managing dependencies, proactively mitigating risks, and clearing blockers to ensure predictable, timely delivery.

●      Stakeholder Alignment: Facilitate regular syncs, communication channels, and status updates across technical and non-technical teams to maintain total alignment on system improvements and the overarching operational roadmap.

●      System Optimization: Continuous evaluation of core systems to identify opportunities to maximize application capabilities, scale technical workflows, and improve productivity.

Qualifications and Education Requirements:

●      Experience: 5–7 years of experience managing technical programs, complex software projects, or enterprise systems implementations.

●      Proven track record of translating ambiguous business requests into structured, execution-ready technical documentation, such as system process maps and PRDs.

●      Proficient in utilizing project management and issue-tracking platforms (e.g., JIRA) to drive engineering workflows and sprint cycles.

●      Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to listen intently, influence stakeholders, and communicate technical concepts.

●      Strong ability to multitask, prioritize competing high-impact initiatives, and manage time effectively within a fast-moving, high-growth environment.

●      A dedicated team player who excels at building consensus and driving collaboration across distinct internal teams.

Preferred Skills:

●      Strong quantitative and data analysis skills, with experience leveraging system telemetry and application usage data and making data-driven roadmap decisions.

●      Experience creating low-fidelity UX wireframes or interface mockups to effectively visualize user workflows and guide frontend adjustments.

Additional Notes:

Target cash compensation range: $104,700 - $125,200 USD Annually

jobs.title: Regional Insurance Product Manager (Remote, US)
jobs.jd_full:
Why Openly

Openly is rebuilding insurance from the ground up. We are re-envisioning and enhancing every aspect of the customer experience. Doing this requires a rapidly growing team of exceptional, curious, empathetic people with a wide range of skill sets, spanning technology, data science, product, marketing, sales, service, claims handling, finance, etc.

The Openly Difference

We created Openly because we saw an evident gap in the market for premium insurance made simple. Consumers deserve more complete coverage at competitive prices.

  • The Price Difference: Using cutting-edge data and technology, we provide you with customizable, competitive prices to protect your most valuable assets.
  • The Policy Difference: Coverages are truly customizable to meet your individual protection needs, for both standard coverages and optional add-ons.
  • The Experience Difference: From tailored claims handling to highly responsive customer service, we are focused on making the home insurance purchasing process a better overall experience.

Welcome to your next adventure.

At Openly, our people are just as important as our product. For us, collaboration, communication, and work-life balance are more than nice-to-haves— they’re the must-haves that make us who we are. We believe a great company is the result of a shared set of values, so we look for these qualities in every candidate we hire.

  • Integrity
  • Empathy
  • Teamwork
  • Curiosity
  • Urgency

We’ve designed our hiring process with you, the candidate, in mind. At every step, you have the chance to present your strengths and learn more about what makes Openly a great place to work.

We’re committed to Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion

We embrace individuality and believe diverse teams are winning teams. Our commitment to inclusion across race, gender, age, religion, identity, and experience drives us forward every day.

Job Details

We are seeking a Regional Insurance Product Manager to own state-level strategy and market performance across an assigned region, and to help bring Openly insurance products to independent agents across the country. Reporting to the Director of Insurance Product Management, this highly visible role offers the opportunity to lead key insurance product management initiatives and interact with business leaders across Technology and Operations Teams. This role requires a self-motivated, high-energy individual who can efficiently function in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment.

  • Developing cost-benefit analyses of state-level strategic initiatives and presenting recommendations that balance market opportunity with company objectives and profitability targets
  • Identifying business challenges and opportunities, designing analytical frameworks to evaluate options, and delivering sound recommendations
  • Cultivating partnerships with Business Development, serving as the primary conduit for field-level market feedback into the Insurance Product organization
  • Escalating state-level insights that warrant countrywide product changes, acting as an internal advocate for market needs

jobs.title: Senior Market Operations Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
At Counterpart Health (CPH), we are transforming healthcare and improving patient care with our innovative primary care tool, Counterpart Assistant. By supporting Primary Care Physicians (PCPs), we are able to deliver improved outcomes to our patients at a lower cost through early diagnosis and longitudinal care management of chronic conditions.

The CPH Program Manager will own the performance and outcomes of our provider engagement and growth programs, shaping how they run, surfacing risks early, and making the calls that keep them on track.. You will keep a real-time pulse on how specific programs, initiatives and maturity stages are performing, translate that into concise reporting for leadership, escalate issues before they become blockers, and be comfortable picking up the phone with practice staff and providers when the team needs an extra set of hands for outreach or follow-up.

As a  Senior Market Operations Program Manager, you will:

  • Program ownership: Own the end-to-end operating model and outcomes for provider engagement and growth teams programs, initiatives and roll outs, including timelines, milestones, dependencies, and whether the program delivers their intended results. Design how these workstreams run, keep them aligned and moving, and make the calls needed to hit the outcomes leadership is counting on.
  • Lead cross functional teams and drive progress through influence, data and timelines.
  • Analytics & leadership decision support: Track program status and performance through various data sources. Translate the numbers into concise, on-cadence updates for senior leadership. Provide recommendations for the priority calls, tradeoffs, and mitigations leadership should make, and influence where the programs focus next.
  • Issue flagging & escalation: Spot risks and blockers early and route them to the right owner. Maintain clear escalation pathways so decisions happen fast and the right stakeholders are looped in at the right time.
  • External stakeholder engagement: Fill gaps as needed on growth and provider engagement work, including initial outreach to practices and providers, follow-up on engagement touchpoints, and direct conversations with practice staff and providers to keep momentum.
  • Cross-functional coordination: Partner with market operations, growth, provider engagement, analytics, and commercial teams to align programs on strategy and direction. Lead through influence across teams you don’t own, drive decisions to resolution, and hold the group accountable to outcomes.
  • Operating model & process design: Design the operating model and playbooks that let provider engagement and growth programs scale, setting the standards and practices other teams follow rather than only documenting what already exists.

Success in this role looks like:

  • First 30 days: You’ve built a working understanding of the in-flight provider engagement and growth programs, met the key internal and external stakeholders, and mapped how status, risks, and decisions currently flow to leadership.
  • By 60 days: You’ve stood up a consistent reporting cadence for senior leadership, taken ownership of 2–3 in-flight programs, accountable for their outcomes and making the priority calls to keep them on track, while  closing gaps in provider outreach and follow-up directly where needed.
  • By 90 days: Leadership has a reliable, weekly view into program health. Escalation pathways are documented and being used. Providers and practice staff hear from someone consistently, even when the primary owner is unavailable.
  • Longer term: You’ve become the operating spine for CPH’s provider engagement and growth programs, the person leadership trusts to set priorities, shape how programs run  and what needs to move next.

You should get in touch if:

  • You’re exceptionally organized and are energized by keeping many moving pieces on track without losing detail.
  • You have experience building and monitoring physician network programs and/or clinical operations
  • Healthcare or value-based care is a must, and PMP certification or an MBA is preferred.
  • You’re fluent with data, comfortable pulling, interpreting, and communicating program metrics from various tools like CRMs, and turning them into clear narratives for senior leaders.
  • You have strong judgment on when to escalate, and you’re comfortable pushing an issue upward when it needs attention.
  • You’re confident and warm with external stakeholders, able to pick up outreach to a practice, follow up with a provider, or run a check-in call and represent CPH well.
  • You bring 5+ years of program management, operations, or client-facing experience.
  • You thrive in a fast-moving, early-stage environment where priorities shift and speed matters.

Benefits Overview:

  • Financial Well-Being: Our commitment to attracting and retaining top talent begins with a competitive base salary and equity opportunities. Additionally, we offer a performance-based bonus program, 401k matching, and regular compensation reviews to recognize and reward exceptional contributions.
  • Physical Well-Being: We prioritize the health and well-being of our employees and their families by providing comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage. Your health matters to us, and we invest in ensuring you have access to quality healthcare.
  • Mental Well-Being: We understand the importance of mental health in fostering productivity and maintaining work-life balance. To support this, we offer initiatives such as No-Meeting Fridays, monthly company holidays, access to mental health resources, and a generous flexible time-off policy. Additionally, we embrace a remote-first culture that supports collaboration and flexibility, allowing our team members to thrive from any location.
  • Professional Development: Developing internal talent is a priority for Clover. We offer learning programs, mentorship, professional development funding, and regular performance feedback and reviews.

Additional Perks:

  • Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) offering discounted equity opportunities
  • Reimbursement for office setup expenses
  • Monthly cell phone & internet stipend
  • Remote-first culture, enabling collaboration with global teams
  • Paid parental leave for all new parents
  • And much more!

About Counterpart Health: In 2018, Clover Health set out to do something unprecedented: build a clinically intuitive, AI-enabled solution that fits within physicians’ workflows to help support the earlier diagnosis and management of chronic conditions.

Years later, that vision is a reality, with thousands of practitioners using Counterpart Assistant during patient visits to improve disease management, reduce medical expenses, and drive success in value-based care.

With an exceptional team of value-based care and technology experts, Counterpart Health is driving value-based care at the speed of software.

Counterpart Health is a subsidiary of Clover Health. From Clover’s inception, Diversity & Inclusion have always been key to our success. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and our employees are people with different strengths, experiences, perspectives, opinions, and backgrounds, who share a passion for improving people’s lives. Diversity not only includes race and gender identity, but also age, disability status, veteran status, sexual orientation, religion and many other parts of one’s identity. All of our employee’s points of view are key to our success, and inclusion is everyone’s responsibility.

$107,000—$140,000 USD

jobs.title: Sr. Product Manager - Data Insights
jobs.jd_full:
HHAeXchange is the leading technology platform for home and community-based care. Founded in 2008, HHAeXchange was born out of an idea to create a fully comprehensive end-to-end homecare solution to help people who are aging or have disabilities thrive in their homes and communities. Our employees are passionate about transforming the healthcare space by building the only homecare ecosystem that fully connects patients, personal care providers, managed care organizations, and states.

The Sr Product Manager  is responsible for maximizing the success of their products by analysis, planning and execution throughout the entire product lifecycle.  This includes gathering and prioritizing market/product requirements, defining the product vision and goals, and collaborating with Product Management leadership, Product Development, Sales and Marketing, and Delivery teams to deliver successful products and ensure revenue and client satisfaction goals are met.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential job duty satisfactorily.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

This is a fully remote opportunity for candidates located in the EST or CST time zones within the US only.

Essential Job Duties

  • Define and execute the product strategy for enterprise data insights leveraging Snowflake Cloud Data Platform.
  • Partner with Data Engineering, Analytics, AI/ML, and business stakeholders to transform enterprise data into actionable insights.
  • Identify opportunities to unlock business value through self-service analytics, ad hoc reporting, dashboards, and predictive models.
  • Drive the product roadmap for enterprise reporting, data exploration, and advanced analytics capabilities.
  • Evaluate and recommend modern BI, analytics, and AI tools that complement Snowflake to maximize business value (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Sigma, ThoughtSpot, dbt, Databricks, Cortex AI, etc.).

  • Conduct regular product roadmap reviews, product demos, and product advisory workshops with internal and external stakeholders.

  • Partner with Product Management and Technology Leadership on long-term strategic initiative planning and resource alignment.

  • Expert-level communication skills for eliciting and understanding customer needs, conveying vision, authoring complex product requirements, and delivering crisp market-facing documents and presentations.
  • Ability to develop strong working relationships and assert influence across departments to manage stakeholder expectations and drive the overall success of the product.
  • In-depth understanding of Agile Product Management and Development principles.  Classroom Agile/Scrum training or certification a plus.
  • Familiarity with SaaS business model and associated product health metrics and configuration concepts.

jobs.title: Staff Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
About Phaidra

Phaidra is building the future of industrial automation.

The world today is filled with static, monolithic infrastructure. Factories, power plants, buildings, etc. operate the same they’ve operated for decades — because the controls programming is hard-coded. Thousands of lines of rules and heuristics that define how the machines interact with each other. The result of all this hard-coding is that facilities are frozen in time, unable to adapt to their environment while their performance slowly degrades.

Phaidra creates AI-powered control systems for the industrial sector, enabling industrial facilities to automatically learn and improve over time. Specifically:

  • We use reinforcement learning algorithms to provide this intelligence, converting raw sensor data into high-value actions and decisions.
  • We focus on industrial applications, which tend to be well-sensorized with measurable KPIs — perfect for reinforcement learning.
  • We enable domain experts (our users) to configure the AI control systems (i.e. agents) without writing code. They define what they want their AI agents to do, and we do it for them.

Our team has a track record of applying AI to some of the toughest problems. From achieving superhuman performance with DeepMind’s AlphaGo, to reducing the energy required to cool Google’s Data Centers by 40%, we deeply understand AI and how to apply it in production for massive impact.

Phaidra’s ability to achieve its mission is determined by our ability to work together — as defined by our core values: Transparency, Collaboration, Operational Excellence, Ownership, and Empathy. We seek individuals who embody these values, as they are instrumental in ensuring our team consistently delivers excellence and fosters an engaging and supportive culture

Phaidra is based in the USA, but we are 100% remote with no physical office. We hire employees internationally with the help of our partner, OysterHR. Our team is currently located throughout the USA, Canada, UK, Sweden, Spain, Portugal, the Netherlands, Singapore, Australia, and India.

Who You Are

We are looking for a Staff Product Manager to lead one of the most important product bets at Phaidra: making Prism the connective data surface for the entire data center operation. It’s the system operators trust for diagnostics and triage, and the layer executives rely on for operating intelligence across the site. Working closely with the CPO, you will shape and drive the strategy that extends Prism’s reach from the floor to the boardroom.

You are bold and creative, with deep empathy for the operators on the floor who feel the pain first, and equal fluency with the executives who buy platforms. You spend time on-site and on calls with customers regularly, not just in research readouts, and you can tell me the last specific problem you heard from one. You think in systems and flywheels, not features. You are comfortable helping shape a big, opinionated bet and then sequencing the pragmatic steps that get there. You partner naturally with Engineering, AI, Design, and go-to-market, and you can hold a technical architecture discussion and a pricing-strategy discussion in the same afternoon.

**We are seeking teammates who are based in Eastern or Central time zones (US or Canada).

Responsibilities

You will partner with the CPO to develop and drive the product strategy and roadmap that establishes Prism as the agentic operating platform for the data center — spanning diagnostics, triage, and the broader intelligence layer operators and executives depend on.

Concretely, you will:

  • Work with the CPO to translate the platform vision into a sequenced, fundable roadmap, and drive it to delivery across teams.
  • Regularly visit and join calls with customers to see Prism in production and hear unfiltered feedback, not just what’s escalated.
  • Translate floor-level customer pain into clear, high-quality product requirements, keeping teams close to real operator context.
  • Help drive expansion along two axes — connecting more of the data center’s operational and business systems, and advancing our insight & recommendations and supervised & autonomous control offerings.
  • Lead the delivery of key integrations that connect new systems into Prism, closing the loop from detection to diagnosis to action.
  • Partner with Engineering and Design to sequence and ship the platform capabilities that make broader, safer autonomy possible.
  • Support the expansion of Prism’s user base beyond the operations floor to new personas and buyers, including facilities and infrastructure leadership at hyperscale and neocloud operators, and partner with go-to-market and Finance on packaging and pricing.
  • Establish frameworks for prioritization, discovery, and risk mitigation; build high-quality product artifacts; coach and mentor other PMs, and help raise the standard for product practice — frameworks, artifacts, discovery rigor — across the team, not just within your own initiatives.
  • Own the metrics that define success in this role, including adoption across new systems and personas, progress along the insight-to-autonomy axis, and expansion revenue, and use them to guide prioritization and roadmap trade-offs.
  • Deeply understand the product architecture and participate actively in technical planning, so the strategy stays grounded in what the platform can actually do.
  • Help ensure alignment across teams by clearly communicating strategy, decisions, and trade-offs.

Key Qualifications

  • 8+ years of technical product management experience, including leading large, complex, cross-functional initiatives.
  • Comfortable spending significant time directly with customers or end users, and treats that time as core product work, not a distraction from it.
  • Track record of helping take a product from point solution toward platform — or contributing to a platform, integration, or data-surface product.
  • Exceptional ability to translate customer needs into strategic product direction and to sequence an ambitious vision into fundable steps.
  • Deep expertise in prioritization frameworks and product decision-making under uncertainty.
  • Strong technical acumen, including system architecture, integrations/APIs, and data platforms.
  • Ability to influence at all levels, including leadership, and to align teams on strategy, decisions, and trade-offs.
  • Experience mentoring and developing other product managers.
  • Excellent communication skills, including explaining complex ideas to audiences from the floor to the boardroom, in an all-remote setting.
  • Shares our company values: Collaboration, Transparency, Operational Excellence, Ownership, & Empathy.

Preferred Skills & Experience

  • Domain experience in data centers, critical infrastructure, industrial, or energy systems.
  • Experience with forward-deployed or services-led motions and turning them into platform revenue.
  • Familiarity with operational systems in this space, such as BMS/EPMS, CMMS, DCIM, or ITSM.
  • Experience productizing agentic AI or autonomous/closed-loop control.
  • Experience designing layered or usage-based B2B pricing, and familiarity with B2B sales and go-to-market.
  • Startup or high-growth experience with distributed/global teams.

Relevant Systems & Technologies

  • Prism platform and its knowledge base
  • Agentic control agents
  • BMS/EPMS telemetry
  • MQTT and other industrial communication protocols
  • Data center operational and business systems (e.g., CMMS, DCIM, ITSM)
  • GCP-based cloud stack

Onboarding

In your first 30 days…

  • Complete onboarding and immerse yourself in Prism, the strategy, and how a customer install works end-to-end today.
  • Spend time with operators to see the floor-level pain and the diagnostics/triage workflows Prism supports now.
  • Deeply understand our customer base and their major pain points.
  • Deeply understand your partners across Engineering, Design, Sales, and Leadership.

In your first 60 days…

  • Develop an informed point of view on roadmap sequencing and pressure-test it with the CPO, Engineering, and Design.
  • Take ownership of an early, high-leverage integration or capability, from requirements through delivery planning.
  • Join multiple live customer implementation and support calls.
  • Build the relationships across teams that you’ll need to drive a cross-functional bet.

In your first 90 days…

  • Partner with the CPO to publish a sequenced roadmap with clear near-term moves.
  • Have direct conversations with at least two major customer accounts to inform the roadmap and direction.
  • Land said high-leverage integration or expanded capability with a live customer deployment, thereby moving Prism one concrete step closer towards the platform direction.
  • Contribute to an initial pricing or packaging experiment with a friendly account.

General Interview Process

All of our interviews are held via Google Meet, and an active camera connection is required.

  1. Meeting with Operations (30 minutes) — Meet you, learn about your background, and cover formalities around your application.
  2. Hiring Manager interview (45 minutes) — Your experience and technical background, and a chance to ask about the team and role.
  3. Execution & Technical Depth (60 minutes) — Prioritization, requirements, and cross-functional execution, plus a discussion of the underlying architecture.
  4. Culture fit interview with one of Phaidra’s co-founders (30 minutes) — Alignment with Phaidra’s values and mutual cultural fit.

Base Salary

US Residents:

  • Tier 1 (Largest highest-cost metros): 176,800 USD - 243,100 USD
  • Tier 2 (Other major metros): 167,960 USD - 230,945 USD
  • Tier 3 (Mid-sized metro areas): 159,120 USD - 218,790 USD
  • Tier 4 (All other locations): 150,280 USD - 206,635 USD

Canada Residents:

  • Tier 1 (Vancouver): 185,925 CAD - 255,648 CAD
  • Tier 2 (Toronto): 173,530 CAD - 238,604 CAD
  • Tier 3 (Montreal): 148,741 CAD - 204,519 CAD
  • Tier 4 (Smaller cities / rural areas): 136,346 CAD - 187,472 CAD

In addition to base salary, this position is eligible for equity. Final salary will be determined based on several factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies, experience, expertise, education and location. In some cases, final compensation may fall outside the posted range. Salary ranges are regularly reviewed and may be adjusted in response to market trends.

Benefits & Perks

  • Fast-paced, team-oriented environment where your work directly shapes the company’s direction.
  • We are a 100% remote company.
  • Competitive compensation & meaningful equity.
  • Outsized responsibilities & professional development.
  • Training is foundational; functional, customer immersion, and development training.
  • Medical, dental, and vision insurance (exact benefits vary by region).
  • Unlimited paid time off, with a required minimum of 20 days per year.
  • Paid parental leave (exact benefits vary by region).
  • Flexible stipends to support your workspace, well-being, and continued professional development.
  • Company MacBook.

Please note: Not all of Phaidra’s benefits and perks listed above apply to temporary employees such as interns.

On being Remote

We take a thoughtful and intentional approach to remote collaboration. Inspired by pioneers like GitLab, we embrace proven best practices to foster an exceptional remote work environment. Our culture is documentation-first, and we prioritize asynchronous communication to support focus and flexibility across time zones. While we value independence, we stay closely connected through tools like Slack and video conferencing. Weekly all-hands meetings help us align and build strong relationships, and we regularly host virtual team-building activities and social events to maintain a sense of camaraderie.

Equal Opportunity Employment

We welcome diversity and strive to maintain an inclusive environment for all employees. If you need assistance with completing the application process, please contact us at hiring@phaidra.ai.

jobs.title: Product Manager, Planning
jobs.jd_full:
About Us

Planful is the pioneer of financial performance management cloud software. The Planful platform, which helps businesses drive peak financial performance, is used around the globe to streamline business-wide planning, budgeting, consolidations, reporting, and analytics. Planful’s award-winning AI capabilities accelerate decision-making, helping Planful users in over 100 countries to close faster, accelerate cycle times, increase productivity, and improve accuracy. Planful is a private company with more than 1,500 customers, including Bose, Boston Red Sox, Five Guys, Grafton Plc, Gousto, and Specialized, and is backed by Vector Capital, a leading global private equity firm. Learn more at Planful.com.

About the role

Planful is seeking a full-time Product Manager, Planning to join our world-class product team in Hyderabad, India. Reporting to the SVP of Product, this individual will own the roadmap and execution for Planful’s dynamic planning capabilities—driving continuous, connected, and AI-powered planning experiences for finance and operations teams across the enterprise. The right candidate combines deep domain knowledge in financial and operational planning with a passion for building intelligent, modern software. You will be responsible for gathering requirements, translating them into actionable stories, and delivering high-quality products to the enterprise performance management market. If you want to move fast, shape the future of AI-forward planning software, and make a real impact—this is the role for you.

Requirements

  • 3–5+ years of experience as a Product Manager, Program Manager, or consultant with enterprise software, preferably in FP&A, CPM, EPM, or related planning and analytics domains.
  • Deep domain knowledge in financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and operational planning—ideally with hands-on experience as a user, implementation consultant, or PM of planning software.
  • Demonstrated experience incorporating or working alongside AI/ML capabilities in a product context, including familiarity with LLMs, predictive modeling, or AI-assisted workflows.
  • Actively uses AI tools (e.g., generative AI, co-pilots, automated analysis) to improve personal productivity and bring innovative ideas to product development.
  • Strong understanding of driver-based and continuous planning methodologies, rolling forecasts, scenario and sensitivity analysis, and workforce/headcount planning.
  • Experience defining user interface concepts for complex business applications, including wireframes, mockups, and final UX decision-making.
  • Understanding of EPM concepts including financial analytics and visualization, data integration, multi-dimensional modeling, and financial reporting.
  • Proven ability to define and communicate product requirements to a broad audience—engineers, QA, marketing, senior management, customers, and analysts—through both verbal and written communication.
  • Proficiency with modern product management tools: Atlassian Jira, Claude, Google Docs, Figma, Pendo or similar.
  • Experience with SaaS development models, cloud computing, and Agile/Scrum development methodologies.
  • Undergraduate degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Intelligence, Computer Science, or related field. Graduate degree is a plus.
  • Independent thinker and self-starter who operates with urgency and can research, synthesize, and present findings with minimal direction.
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills, including comfort presenting to large audiences in person or over video, and ability to craft clear requirements and specification documents.
  • Experience working with or presenting to U.S.-based customers and stakeholders.
  • Experience in a lead role mentoring or managing team members is a plus.

Responsibilities

  • Define and own the product roadmap for Planful’s dynamic planning capabilities and set engineering priorities accordingly.
  • Write and share clear functional specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria with development, QA, and UX teams.
  • Design and champion intuitive new planning functionality in close partnership with engineering and UX teams, ensuring an exceptional user experience for finance and operations users.
  • Evangelize product vision, planning strategy, and AI capabilities to customers, partners, prospects, and internal stakeholders including Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success.
  • Integrate AI capabilities into the planning product—including predictive analytics, intelligent scenario modeling, anomaly detection, and natural language interfaces—to deliver an AI-forward user experience.
  • Leverage AI tools and techniques personally to accelerate research, synthesis, prototyping, and requirements development workflows.
  • Analyze the competitive landscape for planning and FP&A solutions and define differentiated requirements that advance Planful’s market position.
  • Evaluate product performance and feature utility with internal stakeholders (support, services, sales) and external stakeholders (customers, partners, analysts).
  • Lead research and discovery in areas of innovation, including AI-driven planning automation, rolling forecasts, scenario planning, and workforce/operational planning.
  • Define and refine the data model and calculation layer requirements to support flexible, multi-dimensional planning across finance and operations.
  • Partner with the broader product team to ensure coherent integration between planning, reporting, consolidation, and analytics capabilities within the Planful platform.

Why Planful

Planful exists to enrich the world by helping our customers and our people achieve peak performance. To foster the best in class work we’re so proud of, we’ve created a best in class culture, including:

  • Volunteer days, Birthday PTO, and company-wide Wellness Days
  • Supply of diapers and meal deliveries for the first month of your Maternity/Paternity leave
  • Office hubs in Hyderabad and Indore. Employees also receive benefits such as a home office setup allowance, as well as monthly reimbursements for Wi-Fi and cell phone expenses.
  • Join Employee Resource Groups like Women of Planful, Parents of Planful, and more. We celebrate individuality and encourage teammates to bring their authentic selves to work - with full support for creating new ERGs and communities along the way
  • Participate in People Programs that foster connection and wellbeing, such as Movember, Summer Wellness, Breast Cancer Awareness Month, and Hispanic Heritage Month

jobs.title: PMO Project Manager - Investment Management
jobs.jd_full:
About us

At Davy, it’s the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team – you are a key player in shaping our future.

At Davy, you are the difference.

Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high-net-worth individuals, self-directed investors, small-to-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic & international institutional investors.

Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally.

About you

jobs.title: Strategic Technical Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post.

About the Role

CoLab’s Product team is looking for a Technical Program Manager (TPM) to lead the successful execution of complex R&D deliverables for our largest enterprise customers. This role will report directly into the Director of Technical Program Management, with strong cross-functional partnership with Customer Success and Product to ensure a best-in-class customer experience. While accountable for product delivery outcomes, the TPM will be core member of the customer account team and play a key role in driving trust, transparency, and long term success with CoLab.

You’ll serve as the primary point person for contractual product deliverables, coordinating across Engineering, Product, and the Success team and overseeing the full delivery cycle from scoping to execution. This customer-facing role requires clear communication and executive presence when engaging with both internal and customer stakeholders.

You’ll ensure that product managers, customer success managers, engineers, and CoLab leadership are aligned on what’s being delivered, by when, and how it connects back to customer outcomes. You’ll lead program planning, manage delivery milestones, track execution, and surface risk early, navigating competing priorities and negotiating deliverables, timelines, and scope with senior stakeholders on both sides. A strong understanding of agile software development is essential.

In the first 6–12 months, success looks like:

  • Establishing a scalable model for managing customer product deliverables
  • Leading multiple enterprise programs end-to-end without needing executive triage
  • Building trust with Product Managers and Engineers while driving accountability to contractual outcomes
  • Establishing a regular cadence of crisp, status-oriented project updates that build confidence with customers
  • Making it easy for leadership to see program health, risks, and resource alignment across teams

Our Ideal Candidate

You’ve managed complex technical programs in a B2B SaaS or enterprise software setting, with extensive outward customer communication. You think in systems and edge cases, but communicate with clarity. You’re familiar with agile software development and comfortable speaking the language of engineers and product managers. You’re energized by getting things organized, but don’t get stuck in process for process’ sake.

You’re a proactive communicator who makes it easy for others to know what’s happening and where they need to plug in, particularly with cross-functional stakeholders. You’re adept at mining for conflict, a strong negotiator, and identify and resolve issues before they escalate. You’re comfortable leading scoping conversations, user testing workflows, and feedback cycles, and you thrive on closing the loop between customer expectation and product delivery.

This role won’t be a fit if you shy away from ambiguity, hesitate to push back when priorities collide, or wait to be told what to do.

Job Responsibilities

  • Own end-to-end internal coordination of customer product deliverables across Product, Engineering, and Success, surfacing blockers and risk early
  • Collaborate with Product Managers to ensure deliverables are fully scoped, resourced, and sequenced within the roadmap
  • Partner with CSMs to align internal delivery timelines with customer expectations, identifying risks and dependencies to proactively problem solve
  • Lead and operationalize the user acceptance testing (UAT) process, coordinating customer participation, success criteria, and timelines
  • Collect, synthesize, and communicate post-delivery feedback to drive improvement and inform future roadmap decisions
  • Define and track success metrics that align delivery milestones with customer value realization (ex: activation, first value, adoption)
  • Build and manage tools and processes to track program scope, delivery status, risk, and decision history
  • Create and own customer-facing communications including executive reviews around program updates, status, and scope changes to ensure messaging, tone, and cadence meet enterprise expectations

Qualifications

Must-Haves:

  • 5+ years of experience in program management or customer-facing project/program roles in SaaS
  • Proven ability to lead complex, multi-stakeholder programs across technical and customer teams
  • Strong understanding of agile software development processes (e.g., sprint planning, backlog grooming, delivery rhythms)
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to write crisp updates and facilitate tough conversations
  • Highly organized, proactive, and outcomes-driven—you know how to bring structure to chaos

Bonus Points:

  • Experience working with Fortune 1000 or large enterprise customers
  • Familiarity with B2B SaaS workflows, especially in the engineering or manufacturing tech space

Extra Details

  • Compensation: This is a full-time, permanent position with an attractive compensation package that includes stock options.
  • Benefits: This role offers an extended health and benefits package that includes unlimited paid vacation and RRSP or 401K  matching
  • Remote/Hybrid Work: This role has the flexibility to work from anywhere within North America, with preference given to candidates located in Pacific Standard Time (PST)

Frequently cited statistics show that people who identify with historically marginalized groups are likely to apply to jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. We encourage you to help us break that statistic and apply even if you don’t meet every single qualification—your potential is what matters most to us.

jobs.title: Service Delivery Manager – Unified Communications & Video Conferencing
jobs.jd_full:
Join New Era Technology, where People First is at the heart of everything we do. With a global team of over 3,000 professionals, we’re committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels valued, empowered, and inspired to grow. Our mission is to securely connect people, places, and information with end-to-end technology solutions at scale.

At New Era, you’ll join a team-oriented culture that prioritizes your personal and professional development. Work alongside industry-certified experts, access continuous training, and enjoy competitive benefits. Guided by our core attributes — putting people first, embracing continuous learning, and thriving through collaboration and inclusion — we nurture our people to deliver exceptional customer service.

If you want to make an impact in a supportive, growth-oriented environment, New Era is the place for you. Apply today and help us shape the future of work—together

Service Delivery Manager

Position Summary

The Service Delivery Manager (SDM) serves as the primary operational and service management lead responsible for ensuring the successful delivery of managed collaboration, video conferencing, Unified Communications (UC), AV, and IT services to the customer. The SDM acts as the key liaison between the customer, service delivery teams, and operational support functions, ensuring service excellence, SLA compliance, continual service improvement, and customer satisfaction throughout the contract lifecycle.

This role also serves as the operational lead for enterprise Video Conferencing and Unified Collaboration Services, ensuring the availability, reliability, and performance of video conferencing platforms across the customer environment. The ideal candidate will have recent hands-on experience managing enterprise video conferencing technologies and collaboration services within a large enterprise or managed services environment.

The SDM will oversee service operations, manage escalations, coordinate cross-functional teams, lead service reviews, and drive process improvements aligned with ITIL best practices. This role requires strong stakeholder management, operational governance, service management expertise, and the ability to drive customer-focused outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Service Delivery & Operations Management

  • Own and manage the overall service relationship between the customer and service delivery teams.

  • Monitor day-to-day service operations and ensure services are delivered according to agreed SLAs and contractual commitments.

  • Review operational performance metrics and service reports to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement.

  • Coordinate multiple support teams to ensure seamless service delivery.

  • Act as the primary escalation point for service-related issues and operational challenges.

  • Manage the operational delivery of enterprise Video Conferencing, Unified Communications (UC), and Collaboration Services.

  • Ensure the availability, reliability, and performance of enterprise video conferencing platforms across global meeting environments.

  • Coordinate AV, Collaboration, Networking, and Operations teams to resolve conferencing-related incidents and service issues.

  • Monitor conferencing platform health, utilization, service quality, and user experience metrics.

  • Support executive meetings, business-critical conferencing events, and major collaboration initiatives when required.

  • Oversee operational readiness for conferencing platform upgrades, migrations, and new technology deployments.

Service Level Management

  • Monitor and review SLA achievement and operational KPIs.

  • Investigate service performance issues and coordinate corrective actions.

  • Ensure timely resolution of SLA breaches and service-impacting incidents.

  • Manage service credits and remediation activities related to missed SLAs.

  • Prepare and present service performance reports to customer stakeholders.

  • Track Video Conferencing and Collaboration service performance metrics, incident trends, platform availability, and customer satisfaction.

Customer Relationship Management

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with customer stakeholders.

  • Lead Monthly Service Reviews (MSRs) and operational review meetings.

  • Participate in Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs).

  • Facilitate customer planning, service enhancement, and issue-resolution sessions.

  • Drive customer satisfaction and customer experience initiatives.

  • Partner with customer stakeholders to improve collaboration and video conferencing services.

  • Provide recommendations for improving meeting experiences, conferencing reliability, and user adoption across collaboration platforms.

Continual Service Improvement (CSI)

  • Identify opportunities for service enhancement and operational efficiency.

  • Develop and track continual service improvement plans.

  • Analyze service trends, customer feedback, and operational data to recommend improvements.

  • Drive implementation of approved service improvement initiatives.

  • Analyze conferencing service trends, recurring incidents, and customer feedback to improve collaboration services and operational efficiency.

ITIL Process Governance

Ensure adherence to ITIL service management processes and operational policies.

Govern and support:

  • Incident Management

  • Problem Management

  • Event Management

  • Change Management

  • Request Fulfillment

  • Asset Management

  • Service Level Management

  • Availability Management

  • Capacity Management

  • IT Service Continuity Management

  • Governance and Compliance

  • Continual Service Improvement

Resource & Capacity Planning

  • Support resource planning activities to ensure adequate service coverage.

  • Assess service capacity requirements and provide recommendations.

  • Collaborate with operational teams to forecast demand and resource needs.

  • Coordinate resource planning for enterprise collaboration and conferencing support teams.

Documentation & Compliance

  • Maintain and enforce operational procedures and customer-specific service documentation.

  • Ensure operational processes are documented within the Customer Service Guide (CSG).

  • Drive compliance with customer standards, governance requirements, and service delivery processes.

  • Maintain documentation for collaboration services, conferencing platforms, operational procedures, and customer-specific standards.

Stakeholder & Team Coordination

  • Coordinate activities across internal support teams, vendors, and customer stakeholders.

  • Resolve process integration challenges between operational teams.

  • Provide input to training programs and operational readiness initiatives.

  • Support onboarding and transition activities for new services.

  • Collaborate closely with AV Engineers, Collaboration Engineers, VNOC teams, Network Engineers, Microsoft Teams, Zoom, Cisco Webex, and Service Desk teams to deliver high-quality conferencing services.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Mandatory Skills

  • Minimum 5+ years of Service Delivery Management, IT Operations Management, or IT Service Management experience.

  • Strong knowledge of ITIL Service Management processes and best practices.

  • Experience managing SLAs, KPIs, operational reporting, and service governance.

  • Proven experience leading customer-facing service reviews and executive presentations.

  • Strong Incident, Problem, Change, and Escalation Management experience.

  • Experience coordinating cross-functional technical and operational teams.

  • Excellent stakeholder management and customer relationship skills.

  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.

  • Experience with service reporting, performance metrics, and trend analysis.

  • Exceptional verbal and written communication skills.

  • Strong organizational, planning, and project coordination capabilities.

  • Recent hands-on experience supporting enterprise Video Conferencing, Unified Communications (UC), and Collaboration Services.

  • Experience managing enterprise AV and conferencing operations within a Managed Services or Enterprise IT environment.

  • Strong understanding of conferencing operations, meeting room technologies, and collaboration service delivery.

  • Experience working with cross-functional AV, Networking, Infrastructure, Collaboration, and Operations teams.

Preferred Skills

  • Experience supporting enterprise Unified Communications (UC), Video Conferencing, and Collaboration Services.

  • Recent experience supporting enterprise Video Conferencing technologies is highly preferred.

  • Strong knowledge of Zoom Workplace / Zoom Rooms.

  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft Teams and Microsoft Teams Rooms.

  • Strong knowledge of Cisco Webex and Cisco Collaboration Suite.

  • Experience with Poly (Polycom), Crestron, Logitech Rally Systems, and Neat Devices.

  • Knowledge of SIP, H.323, VoIP, and enterprise collaboration infrastructure.

  • Experience supporting AV room technologies and executive conference rooms.

  • Experience with ServiceNow or similar ITSM platforms.

  • Experience supporting large enterprise customers and global service delivery environments.

  • Experience working within a VNOC (Video Network Operations Center) or Collaboration Operations environment is highly desirable.

  • ITIL Foundation, Practitioner, or Intermediate Certification.

Education & Experience

Required

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, Telecommunications, or a related field (or equivalent experience).

  • Minimum 5 years of Service Delivery Management or IT Service Management experience.

Preferred

  • ITIL Certification.

  • PMP, PRINCE2, or Service Management certifications.

  • Experience supporting enterprise collaboration, conferencing, Unified Communications, or managed services environments.

Key Competencies

  • Customer Focus

  • Leadership & Influence

  • Service Excellence

  • Operational Governance

  • Strategic Thinking

  • Continuous Improvement

  • Escalation Management

  • Communication & Presentation Skills

  • Relationship Management
